title: Move and Resize Windows
subtitle: {key:Ctrl-W} {key:H}/{key:J}/{key:K}/{key:L} moves the window. {key:Ctrl-W} {key:+}/{key:-}/{key:<}/{key:>} resizes.
summary: Capital direction keys after Ctrl-W move the *window* to that side of the layout. Ctrl-W + and - resize vertically; < and > horizontally. Ctrl-W = balances all windows.

blocks.text: Once you have splits, you'll want to rearrange them. The capital direction keys move the current window to the edge of the layout, replacing the surrounding splits with a clean side-by-side or top/bottom configuration.

blocks.label: Move window to far left (full height)

blocks.title: Counts on Resize
blocks.text:
All resize commands accept counts. {key:1}{key:0}{key:Ctrl-W}{key:+} grows the window 10 rows. {key:5}{key:Ctrl-W}{key:>} grows 5 columns.

{key:Ctrl-W}{key:_} with a count sets the window's height to *exactly* that count. {key:2}{key:0}{key:Ctrl-W}{key:_} → 20 rows tall. Without a count, it maxes out (using all available height, with `'winminheight'` left for the others).

Split layouts in Vim are tree-structured: vertical splits within horizontal splits within vertical splits. {key:Ctrl-W}{key:H}/{key:J}/{key:K}/{key:L} flatten the tree from the current window's perspective by promoting it to a top-level split.
{key:Ctrl-W}{key:=} is the magic equalizer. After fiddling with sizes, {key:Ctrl-W}{key:=} makes everything proportional again.
